codeReview.summary: The protected-shell centralization, proxy-based stale-cookie recovery, and dashboard route regrouping are directionally correct, but the feature does not fully satisfy the unsafe `return_to` requirement because the shared sanitizer still permits slash-backslash paths that resolve off-origin.

issues.description: `getSafeReturnTo()` only rejects values starting with `//`, so it accepts inputs like `/\\evil.com`. Browser/WHATWG URL resolution normalizes that to `http://evil.com/`, which means the helper fails VAL-AUTH-002's requirement to neutralize unsafe `return_to` values. Both `apps/dashboard/src/app/login/page.tsx:32` and `apps/dashboard/src/components/sign-in-form.tsx:36` trust this helper for redirects, so visiting `/login?return_to=/%5Cevil.com` can escape the app instead of falling back to `/dashboard`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The dashboard worker skill says `ultracite` is a required skill when editing JS/TS/React files, but this worker session never invoked it even though the handoff reported `followedProcedure: true`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/dashboard-parity-worker/SKILL.md` lists `ultracite` under Required Skills, while the transcript skeleton for worker session `e41e15fd-a33c-482f-b983-ca4a1f88cd5e` contains `dashboard-parity-worker`, `midday-reference`, and `agent-browser` usage but zero `"skill": "ultracite"` entries.

summary: Fail: the feature mostly matches the intended protected-shell shape, but unsafe `return_to` sanitization is incomplete and leaves an open-redirect path via slash-backslash input.
